corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: يَشْتَهُونَ is a finite imperfect plural verb; the row needs form, subject agreement, and contextual object/desire relation before an authored hover can be certified
what_failed: A live populated hover reused a dictionary infinitive instead of the exact finite verb contribution.
sarf_lesson: For finite verbs, record aspect, voice, person, number, gender, and form before accepting entry prose.
nahw_lesson: Clause context may choose the English wording, but it cannot turn a finite token into an infinitive hover.
learner_explanation: يَشْتَهُونَ does not mean the dictionary label 'to desire' as a token; it contributes a plural imperfect action such as 'they desire' by context.
drill_prompt: Given يَشْتَهُونَ, identify the imperfect prefix, plural ending, and why 'to desire' is not a token hover.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: يَعْصِمُكَ must preserve the finite verb host and كَ second-person object before a learner-safe hover such as 'protects you' can be certified
what_failed: The hover exposed the root/entry meaning but hid the attached object pronoun.
sarf_lesson: A verb plus object suffix is a composed written token; the suffix belongs in parse, display segments, and learner explanation.
nahw_lesson: The verb-object relation controls the English contribution and may require referent/context review.
learner_explanation: The final كَ means 'you'. A learner should not see only 'to protect' for يَعْصِمُكَ.
drill_prompt: Segment يَعْصِمُكَ into verb host and object suffix, then write the minimum safe hover contribution.

visible_bad_hover: to choose someone as if filling their reservoir with blessings
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: يَجْتَبِيكَ requires exact finite verb analysis plus كَ object suffix before a concise 'chooses you' style hover can be reviewed
what_failed: An entry explanation was shown where the exact token needs the object suffix and finite form visible.
sarf_lesson: Derived/finite verb entries must not be pasted onto tokens without person/number and suffix accounting.
nahw_lesson: Context and object relation decide whether the public wording is 'chooses you' or another authored contribution, but the 'you' cannot disappear.
learner_explanation: يَجْتَبِيكَ contains a final كَ; any rich hover must account for it.
drill_prompt: Explain why يَجْتَبِيكَ is not satisfied by an entry-family definition alone.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: لِتُضَيِّقُوا۟ has lām/purpose or governor review plus a finite plural verb; component evidence from ضاق cannot certify the whole token
what_failed: A component/root family candidate was treated as useful evidence, but the token still lacks whole-token function, mood, and finite-form proof.
sarf_lesson: Component candidates must stay separate from whole-token candidates and cannot weaken the gate.
nahw_lesson: A lām on a verb can govern mood/purpose or carry another function; its contribution must be reviewed before the hover is trusted.
learner_explanation: The written token starts with لِـ and contains a finite verb, so the learner needs more than the host family's meaning.
drill_prompt: For لِتُضَيِّقُوا۟, list the component candidate and then explain why it remains a blocker row.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: لِمَا needs lām relation plus the function of ما and attachment/context before rich certification
what_failed: A readable English string did not prove the token's preposition/function composition or attachment.
sarf_lesson: The written token must be segmented as lām plus mā before sense propagation; an entry-family link is not identity.
nahw_lesson: مَا can be relative, interrogative, conditional, source-like, or other; lām also adds relation and possible governor behavior.
learner_explanation: لِمَا is not just one dictionary word. The lām and mā each contribute something that the hover must explain or block.
drill_prompt: Classify لِمَا in context: what does the lām contribute, and what function does mā have?

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: لَّمَّا needs exact negative/exceptive construction review; do not merge it with لِمَا, temporal لَمَّا, or lexical ل م م noun rows
what_failed: One entry family carried particle/construction, exception, temporal, and lexical-noun readings without exact function gating.
sarf_lesson: Strict surface and shadda/tanwīn separate لَّمَّا, لَمَّا, لِمَا, لَّمًّا, and lexical لَمَمَ rows.
nahw_lesson: The construction decides whether the token is temporal, negative, exceptive, or something else; readable text is not enough.
learner_explanation: The same-looking family can mean very different things. The hover must show the construction or remain pending.
drill_prompt: Compare لِمَا and لَّمَّا and name the gate each needs before public hover trust.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: أَقْوَٰتَهَا requires nominal host plus هَا possessor/reference suffix before a learner-ready hover such as 'its sustenance' can be certified
what_failed: A plausible host gloss did not expose the attached suffix pronoun.
sarf_lesson: Nominal hosts with attached suffixes need the same visible accounting as verb-object suffixes.
nahw_lesson: The suffix referent and construction decide the public wording; the suffix cannot be hidden in private metadata only.
learner_explanation: أَقْوَٰتَهَا ends with هَا, so the token contributes a possessor/reference such as 'its/her'.
drill_prompt: Segment أَقْوَٰتَهَا and explain why host-only 'sustenance' is not rich-certified.
